isae3000_objective: An independent external auditor performs a periodic assurance engagement (e.g. ISAE 3000) over the benchmark controls.
artifact: None — no independent external auditor engaged; this pack is the ISAE 3000 / IOSCO-PRA dry run that PREPARES for one
notes: HONEST GAP. No independent external auditor / ISAE 3000 engagement has occurred. QA_AQUA_REVIEW.md is explicitly an internal-style self-review and itself recommends a genuinely independent second-analyst review. This pack is the dry run that prepares for, but does not constitute, that audit.

isae3000_objective: A complete, independently re-verifiable audit trail binds each published figure to its evidence, methodology and approval.
artifact: ledger/publication_ledger.json (hash-chained records, JSON + SQLite mirror) — re-verifiable via build/build_ledger.py --verify
notes: The strongest control in the pack. Every figure binds to evidence sha256 + methodology commit + method_sha256 + QA + approval and the records are hash-chained, so any retroactive edit is detectable. Independently re-verifiable via `python3 build/build_ledger.py --verify` (the cited artifact) and locked by tests/test_ledger.py. The pre-assurance chain head and base record count are pinned live in this matrix's header.

isae3000_objective: A documented complaints process exists with acknowledgement/resolution SLAs and a retained complaints record.
artifact: docs/policies/COMPLAINTS_POLICY.md + the append-only corrections log (ledger/corrections_log.json, supersedes-hash)
notes: The complaints policy (lodging, SLAs, escalation, 5-year record retention) and the append-only corrections log that a complaint-driven restatement writes to both exist. No intake channel is yet operationally staffed and the log is seeded empty — partial.

iosco_principle: Conflicts of interest / segregation of duties
isae3000_objective: Build/production and approval are performed by independent parties; conflicts of interest are identified and managed.
artifact: docs/policies/CONFLICTS_POLICY.md + the build-actor != approver rule in the approval gate (and corrected_by != approved_by in corrections)
notes: The conflicts policy and the neutrality firewall (METHODOLOGY §8) exist, and segregation is enforced as a write/resolve-time rule across the approval, corrections and registry registries (build_actor != approver). But identities are self-asserted strings, and at v1.0 build and approval are the same single operator — true independence (signed commits / SSO / 4-eyes) is not yet achieved. This is the key organisational gap — partial.

isae3000_objective: Documented internal controls (deterministic build, automated testing, QA framework) operate effectively and are evidenced.
artifact: Deterministic build + the automated test suite (live count in the ledger qa block) + the AQuA-framework review, all bound into the ledger
notes: Strong and demonstrable today: a deterministic, network-free build; an automated test suite whose live pass count is pulled from the ledger qa block; an AQuA-framework review; and fail-closed gates (registry --check, --check-approvals) — all tamper-evident inside the hash chain.

isae3000_objective: Material methodology changes follow a documented procedure with version increment, changelog and stakeholder consultation.
artifact: docs/policies/METHODOLOGY_CHANGE_POLICY.md + the registry's fail-closed change gate (version bump + append-only changelog)
notes: The mechanical change procedure is REAL and enforced — a formula edit without a version bump + changelog entry fails the build closed (methodology/build_registry.py --check). The CONSULTATION limb is documented but aspirational: no external constituency has yet been consulted, so this principle is partial.

isae3000_objective: Where a transaction-based input hierarchy exists, concluded transactions are prioritised over estimates/judgments.
artifact: N/A — no concluded-transaction feed; IMES is forward-looking, prices are HARD/ASSESSED-tagged assessments (METHODOLOGY §5)
notes: HONEST GAP and the defining limitation vs a true PRA. InfraMosaic has no submission/transaction window: IMES sub-scores are assessed forward-looking judgments and component prices are triangulated estimates tagged HARD vs ASSESSED. The HARD/ASSESSED confidence tag is the nearest analogue, but there is no concluded-transaction prioritisation. Roadmap: STRATEGY P3 (structured price-assessment window).

isae3000_objective: The methodology is documented, version-controlled and publicly available, and every published figure binds to it.
artifact: docs/METHODOLOGY.md + methodology/registry.json (every figure's formula_ref resolves to a named, versioned, content-hashed method)
notes: Fully public and reproducible: METHODOLOGY.md (v1.0, git-versioned) plus the methodology registry pin every figure to a method_sha256 carried inside its hash-chained ledger record.

isae3000_objective: Records are retained for a defined period per record class with a tamper-evident immutability mechanism.
artifact: docs/policies/RETENTION_SCHEDULE.md + the corrections retention clock (retention_until = date + N yrs, 5yr floor) + git history + hash chain
notes: Retention is genuinely enforced, not just stated: per-class periods in the schedule, a per-entry retention clock in the corrections log, the append-only ledger_hash_archive, and git history + the publication hash chain make source CSVs, ledger snapshots and approvals tamper-evident.

isae3000_objective: The methodology is reviewed on a defined periodic cadence; review outcomes are recorded.
artifact: Registry review{cadence:annual,last_review,next_review_due} per method + docs/QA_AQUA_REVIEW.md (the first review cycle)
notes: An annual cadence and a queryable due-date driver exist (build_registry.py --review --due), and QA_AQUA_REVIEW.md is the first cycle. The recurring human review is a forward commitment, not yet a demonstrated multi-year track record — partial.
